title: Conditional visibility of items (rows) and/or groups in a board based on user in person column
excerpt: Would it be possible to add a feature that only allows non-owners of a board to see a board row/item if they are assigned to one or more person columns in a row?

posts.author: sschreiner (Sebastian Schreiner)
posts.cookedHtml: Would it be possible to add a feature that only allows non-owners of a board to see a board row/item if they are assigned to one or more person columns in a row?

posts.author: sschreiner (Sebastian Schreiner)
posts.cookedHtml: Some feedback from the Monday team on this item would be much appreciated! It would open up some entirely new use cases for us internally e.g. around invoice processing.

posts.author: Markus (Markus Polap)
posts.cookedHtml:
As an Administrator I would love to have this function.
It would make life easier for Admins and users!

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @Markus & others on this thread - This feature is available now for Enterprise plans. It’s called Item Permissions. If you would like a personal demo of it, you can book a time with me here: Book a call with Polished Geek: Do more with monday․com and I’ll be glad to give you a sneak peek.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: By request on another post, I made a short video demonstrating the new Item Permissions and how it works. Sharing it here: monday.com Item Permissions...

posts.author: Markus (Markus Polap)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello There,

firstly I want to thank you for all the effort.
My company and I are just starting with monday and the Pro licence.
And this might help to convince them in the future.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: You are welcome, @Markus . Item Permissions definitely are a game changer when it comes to organizing your workflows and boards. You mentioned you were just starting out, so if you would ever like to chat about how to do more with monday․com (Pro or any other plan) - let me know!

posts.author: chen1 (陈慧民)
posts.cookedHtml: This is the most critical function I need. It is a pity that it must be the enterprise version. Currently, I have 30 employees and I have been told that the cost is more than 12,000 US dollars.But in China our average salary is only 8,000 US dollars a year.More than 10% of corporate profits,Be sure to cheer, if I know the first time that this feature is not necessarily abandoning the use of monday, but to let my software department use open source software

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
For anyone who would like a private demo and walkthrough of Pro vs. Enterprise features, so you can decide if Enterprise is right for your business, you can book one below with us at Polished Geek.
